references.title: Peter Zumthor — Therme Vals
references.description: Masterful use of natural stone with precise directional lighting. Demonstrates how material texture is revealed through light angle and shadow depth.

references.title: Hélène Binet — Architectural Photography
references.description: Dramatic use of shadow and grain to communicate spatial weight. Low camera angles emphasize material presence.

references.title: Tadao Ando — Church of the Light
references.description: Singular light source as spatial device. Concrete material responds to directional light with subtle tonal gradients.

references.title: Sigurd Lewerentz — St. Peter's, Klippan
references.description: Dark brick with minimal openings. Light becomes sculptural — each window is a compositional decision.

references.title: Studio Mumbai — Palmyra House
references.description: Warm timber with tropical diffused light. Material palette of 2-3 elements creates hierarchy without competition.

references.title: Ludwig Mies van der Rohe — Barcelona Pavilion
references.description: Reflective materials (marble, chrome, glass) under controlled natural light. Demonstrates material-light interaction complexity.

references.title: John Pawson — Minimalist Residential
references.description: Neutral palette with emphasis on spatial proportion. Even, soft lighting reveals volume rather than texture.

references.title: Iwan Baan — Documentary Architectural Photography
references.description: Flat, honest documentation light. Wide angles capture spatial relationships without atmospheric distortion.
